candidate_llm_application: Eddy-hopping agent execution discipline: periodic agent-initiated pauses + hypothetical-execution scout + formal back-out + ferry-angle decision-policy

results.title: Basics of Reading Whitewater — Paddling.com
results.functional_justification: Pure source-domain whitewater reading article. No LLM/agent execution mechanism. Not functionally equivalent to LLM agent eddy-hopping discipline.
results.semantic_justification: Vocabulary overlap on eddy/ferry/scout but target application is human paddler skill, not agent execution. Low cosine.

results.title: 9 Most Important Whitewater Kayak Skills
results.functional_justification: Source-domain whitewater kayak skills list. No agent mechanism.
results.semantic_justification: Source-domain vocabulary; not aligned to agent execution semantics.

results.title: How to Whitewater Kayaking — Kayak Soča
results.functional_justification: Beginner kayaking basics. No agent mechanism.
results.semantic_justification: Source-domain only; minimal alignment with LLM agent execution discipline.

results.title: Imagine-then-Plan: Adaptive Lookahead World Models (2026/1 v2)
results.functional_justification: World-model mental sandbox + adaptive lookahead horizon is operationally near-identical to eddy-hop hypothetical-execution scouting with adaptive K-step lookahead. Periodic 'imagine' phase = eddy pause; lookahead = scout; adaptive horizon = variable K. Strong functional match.
results.semantic_justification: Semantic core (imagine/sandbox/lookahead horizon/adaptive) maps tightly to (hypothetical-scout/K-step/eddy-pause). High cosine.

results.title: Architecting Resilient LLM Agents: Plan-then-Execute (2025/9)
results.functional_justification: Plan-then-Execute pattern with Docker sandbox + re-plan + HITL maps to eddy-pause discipline: pause-plan-commit. State snapshots for re-planning = eddy snapshots; sandbox = scout environment; re-plan = back-out. Strong functional overlap.
results.semantic_justification: Plan-then-execute + sandbox + re-plan semantically mirror eddy-pause + hypothetical-scout + back-out. High cosine.

results.title: Agent Loops to Structured Graphs: Scheduler Framework (2026/4)
results.functional_justification: Separates plan/exec/recover layers; recovery layer = eddy back-out option. Structured scheduler with explicit recovery is functionally close to ferry-angle decision-policy + back-out discipline.
results.semantic_justification: Plan/exec/recover layers semantically align with pause/scout/back-out mechanism. Above threshold.

results.title: SafeAgent: Automated Risk Simulator (2025/5)
results.functional_justification: Automated risk simulator before action commit = hypothetical-execution scouting at action level. Direct mechanism match to eddy-pause hypothetical-scout step. At threshold.
results.semantic_justification: Risk-simulation + automated pre-action evaluation maps to hypothetical-scout + ferry-angle decision-policy. At threshold.

results.title: OpenAgentSafety Framework (2026/2 v2)
results.functional_justification: Containerized sandbox eval framework, but lacks periodic agent-initiated pauses + formal back-out + ferry-angle decision-policy as a combined discipline. Below threshold.
results.semantic_justification: Sandbox/safety eval vocabulary overlaps partially but missing periodicity/back-out specifics. Below threshold.

verdict_reason: total_hits = 5 >= 1. Four LLM-side papers (Imagine-then-Plan world-model lookahead, Plan-then-Execute sandbox + re-plan, plan/exec/recover layered scheduler, SafeAgent automated risk simulator) each independently satisfy semantic and functional thresholds, jointly covering all four legs of the eddy-hopping discipline (periodic pause, hypothetical scout, back-out, ferry-angle decision-policy). One source-domain article also keyword-forced. Candidate is not novel.
notes: Independent re-judging produced the same per-result hit determinations and the same FAIL verdict as 07_hit_miss.json.
